input_text:
Evaluated ClickHouse as the database for our analytics service. ClickHouse is a column-oriented database purpose-built for analytical queries — aggregations over large datasets are extremely fast.

Problem: our analytics data also has OLTP requirements. When an order is placed, we need to update several analytics tables atomically. ClickHouse is optimized for append-only workloads and doesn't support traditional ACID transactions or foreign key relationships.

Keeping PostgreSQL for the analytics tables. ClickHouse would be appropriate if we needed sub-second queries across billions of rows.

expected_negatives.what: ClickHouse as the database for the analytics service
expected_negatives.why: Analytics tables also have OLTP requirements (atomic updates with order placement, foreign keys to orders/users). ClickHouse is optimised for append-only workloads and lacks traditional ACID transactions and FK relationships. Keeping PostgreSQL.

reviewer_notes: Specific workload mismatch ruled out ClickHouse for this service.
